name: Gene Orloff
abstract: Gene Orloff (June 14, 1921 – March 23, 2009) was an American violinist, concertmaster, arranger, contractor and session musician.

about_quotes.speaker: George Handy
about_quotes.evidence: I was fortunate in knowing Gene Orloff who plays violin beautifully... I also had the use of his trumpet... I just took him off the violin.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Watrous
about_quotes.evidence: Gene Orloff and Manny Green and all the big session contractors in New York who suddenly found out that there was somebody besides Urbie Green in New York.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Watrous interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 26, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Albam
about_quotes.evidence: I figured Gene Orloff was going to be the concertmaster... I knew they would be there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Gene Orloff was on it... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, right.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
